lessons.title: What is a block?
lessons.summary: Blocks snap together like puzzle pieces.

payload.title: Blocks, not text
payload.body: Block coding lets you drag blocks into place. Each block is an instruction for the computer.

payload.prompt: What does a block represent?
payload.explanation: A block is one step the computer will do.

payload.prompt: What do we call the color group that moves your sprite?
payload.explanation: Motion blocks change position.

lessons.title: Make the cat move
lessons.summary: Tell the sprite where to go.

payload.title: move 10 steps
payload.body: The "move 10 steps" block nudges your sprite forward by 10 steps.

payload.prompt: Which block moves a sprite?
payload.explanation: move 10 steps changes position.

payload.prompt: Make the sprite move further — fill the number.
payload.explanation: The move block takes a number.

payload.title: Looks blocks
payload.body: The say block shows a speech bubble. Try "say Hello for 2 seconds".

payload.prompt: Which group has the say block?
payload.explanation: say is a Looks block.

payload.prompt: Complete: say ___ for 2 seconds
payload.explanation: say takes text, like "Hi!".

payload.title: forever
payload.body: A forever block wraps other blocks and runs them over and over.

payload.prompt: How many times does forever run?
payload.explanation: forever runs until the green flag is clicked off.

payload.prompt: Wrap the move block so it repeats.
payload.explanation: forever wraps code to loop it.

payload.title: Events
payload.body: The "when flag clicked" block is the start of most projects.

payload.prompt: What triggers the green flag block?
payload.explanation: It runs when the user clicks the green flag.

payload.prompt: Complete: when ___ clicked
payload.explanation: The green flag starts the project.

payload.title: Decisions
payload.body: An "if ___ then" block checks a condition and runs blocks inside when it is true.

payload.prompt: When does the code inside an if-then run?
payload.explanation: Only while the condition is true.

payload.prompt: Check if touching color red.
payload.explanation: The diamond slot holds a true/false block.

payload.title: Make a variable
payload.body: Click "Make a Variable" to create one. Then use "set" or "change" blocks on it.

payload.prompt: Which block adds 1 to a variable called score?
payload.explanation: change ... by N adds to the current value.

payload.prompt: Add 10 to score.
payload.explanation: change score by 10 adds 10.

lessons.title: Make your own block
lessons.summary: Wrap a bunch of blocks into one.

payload.title: My Blocks
payload.body: Custom blocks group steps so your code is tidy. Think of them like naming a recipe.

payload.prompt: Why use a custom block?
payload.explanation: Custom blocks let you reuse and rename logic.

payload.prompt: Which tab creates custom blocks?
payload.explanation: My Blocks is for custom blocks.

lessons.title: Broadcasts
lessons.summary: Let sprites talk to each other.

payload.title: Messages between sprites
payload.body: A broadcast sends a message to every sprite. Other sprites use "when I receive" to react. Great for cutscenes and level changes.

payload.prompt: Which block reacts to a broadcast?
payload.explanation: "when I receive" runs when that message is broadcast.

payload.prompt: Send a message called start.
payload.explanation: broadcast sends the message to everyone.

lessons.title: Clones
lessons.summary: Make copies of a sprite on the fly.

payload.title: create clone of myself
payload.body: Cloning makes a live copy of a sprite that runs its own code. Use "when I start as a clone" to control what a clone does.

payload.prompt: Which block fires on each new clone?
options: when I start as a clone
payload.explanation: Every clone begins with this hat block.

payload.prompt: Make a new clone of myself.
payload.explanation: "myself" clones the sprite you're in.

payload.title: pick random
payload.body: The "pick random 1 to 10" block gives a different whole number each time. Plug it into move, say, or set blocks for surprise.

payload.prompt: What could "pick random 1 to 6" return?
payload.explanation: It's like rolling a 6-sided die.

payload.prompt: Move a random number of steps from 10 to 50.
payload.explanation: Plug the random block into the move slot.

lessons.title: Custom blocks with inputs
lessons.summary: Pass values into your own blocks.

payload.title: Add an input
payload.body: When making a custom block, click "Add an input" to give it a parameter — like distance or color. Inside the block definition, drag the input bubble where you need it.

payload.prompt: Why add an input to a custom block?
options: So the block can use different values each time
payload.explanation: Inputs make your block flexible and reusable.

payload.prompt: Call a block named jump with a height of 80.
payload.explanation: Pass 80 into the input slot.

payload.title: Game loops in Scratch
payload.body: A basic game loop is: when flag clicked, then forever, move the player, check if touching an enemy, change score. This pattern powers most Scratch games.

payload.prompt: Which block runs the loop continuously?
payload.explanation: forever runs the game loop until you stop it.

payload.prompt: Inside forever, check if you hit the enemy sprite.
payload.explanation: Pick the sprite you want to detect.
